case_name: Justin Estess, M.D. v. Forrest Health System, Forrest General Hospital and Pine Grove Behavioral Health & Addiction Services

summary.brief: Justin Estess, M.D. v. Forrest Health System, Forrest General Hospital and Pine Grove Behavioral Health & Addiction Services. Medical malpractice case involving psychiatry.

evidence_index.text: Estess is a licensed physician in Mississippi with a specialization in anesthesiology
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: From February 2017 through June 2017, Estess underwent treatment through Pine Grove’s Next Step and PEP program.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Estess was given a total of seven polygraph tests and two EyeDetect tests.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following Estess’s re-evaluation, Estess said that Pine Grove made a recommendation to MPHP that he was not currently fit to practice medicine
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On June 29, 2020, Estess sent Notice of Claim letters to Andy Woodard, Chief Executive Officer of Forrest Health System and Forrest General Hospital
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court found that FGH met its burden for summary judgment because Estess lacked expert testimony to meet the elements of medical negligence
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Therefore, the trial court did not err when dismissing Estess’s claims of breach of contract and tortious breach of contract.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: To distinguish between malpractice claims and ordinary negligence, a court should consider whether the claim involves a professional relationship
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, the layman’s exception does not apply to “situations involving judgment calls
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was undergoing treatment for opioid addiction and was evaluated for fitness to practice medicine.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Evaluation for fitness to practice medicine

requestables.name: Addiction Treatment Evaluation
requestables.description: Evaluation of patient's progress in addiction treatment

reveal.result_summary: Patient not fit to practice medicine; additional treatment recommended
reveal.clinical_significance: Determines patient's ability to resume medical practice
reveal.decision_impact: Led to extension of treatment and restricted medical license

timeline_phases.name: Addiction Treatment Evaluation
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes evaluation for fitness to practice medicine

decision_points.clinical_context: Evaluation of patient's fitness to practice medicine
decision_points.prompt: What should be the basis for determining fitness to practice?

options.description: Use comprehensive medical and psychological evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Comprehensive evaluation provides a holistic view of patient's readiness

options.description: Rely primarily on polygraph test results
options.clinical_reasoning: Polygraph tests are unreliable for this purpose

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on polygraph test results
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed polygraph tests were sufficient

expected_action_court.description: Comprehensive evaluation should have been used
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established comprehensive evaluation is standard

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Comprehensive evaluation should have been conducted

key_findings: Breach of contract claim dismissed due to lack of evidence
Summary judgment in favor of Forrest General Hospital

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to provide valid contract evidence

court_findings.finding: Summary judgment in favor of Forrest General Hospital
